D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Babinet's Complementary-Screen Principle

swap a slit for a wire of the same width and find the identical side fringes

An aperture and its complement add up to an unobstructed beam. So away from the centre their diffracted fields are equal and opposite — meaning equal intensity.

Set the object width and wavelength, then switch between a slit and the complementary wire. The side fringes overlap exactly; only the central beam differs.
